choice: Pure TS module at lib/engine, no I/O. Seeded RNG = mulberry32 + Box-Muller normals (SeededRng); per-trial seed derived as imul(seed,1000003)+i+1. simulateTrial runs one deterministic annual loop to the youngest person's life expectancy with a TrialHooks seam {computeTax, rmdFloor}; buildHooks wires the real tax+RMD. Yearly order: contributions in -> streams offset spend -> RMD floor (excess reinvested post-tax) -> strategy withdrawal -> income tax paid from assets -> investment growth. Federal tax = 2024 bracket baseline (single + MFJ) with standard deduction, thresholds/deduction scaled by the trial's cumulative inflation factor; state = flat rate on the post-deduction base. RMD = IRS Uniform Lifetime Table, start age 73 (<1960) / 75 (>=1960), max requirement across household persons on the single tax-deferred bucket. runForecast reduces N trials to per-year p10/p25/p50/p75/p90 (linear-interpolated quantiles of total assets) and a per-age success curve = CUMULATIVE survival (P(never insolvent through that age), monotonic non-increasing); headline successProbability = survival through life expectancy. 10k x ~37y runs in ~160ms.
rationale: [build-originated] Decisions 71b1665b/10d3df55/9a5db500/b10161a1/5c68ced9 fixed the WHAT (pure seeded engine, normal draws, real brackets+RMD, three strategies) but not these contracts: RNG algorithm, per-trial seeding, the yearly operation order, tax paid-from-assets vs gross-up, household RMD = max-across-persons, success curve = cumulative survival, and quantile interpolation. A future builder needs these to extend or re-derive Forecasts.
